name: Dolmens of El Pozuelo Route
name_original: Los dólmenes de El Pozuelo — circular
description: Hike the easy 4.3-mile (6.9 km) circular trail around the Dolmens of El Pozuelo, gaining 375 feet (114 metres) in under two hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Zalamea La Real offers trails through varied landscapes, characterized by the historic Río Tinto river valley and surrounding hills. The region features paths that traverse ancient sites, including megalithic dolmens, and follow former railway lines. Many routes provide opportunities for circular walks Zalamea La Real, catering to different experience levels. Best hiking trails around Zalamea La Real The most popular hiking route is Río Tinto Greenway Loop, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the historic Río Tinto. Another top favourite among local hikers is Dolmens of El Pozuelo Route, an easy 4.3 miles (6.9 km) path. This trail explores ancient megalithic structures in the landscape. Local hikers also love the On the banks of the Riotinto along the old railway, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through the Río Tinto river valley, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Hiking around Zalamea La Real is defined by river valleys, historical sites, and local hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents. The routes in Zalamea La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Zalamea La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Zalamea La Real?
A: There are over 40 hiking trails in the Zalamea La Real area, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. This includes 14 easy routes, 23 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging trails.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Zalamea La Real?
A: The trails around Zalamea La Real cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 14 easy routes, perfect for a relaxed stroll, 23 moderate trails for those looking for a bit more challenge, and 4 difficult routes for experienced hikers seeking longer distances and more significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Zalamea La Real?
A: Yes, many routes around Zalamea La Real are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144750763/rio-tinto-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tinto Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1135180731/zalamea-la-real-and-its-hills-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zalamea la Real and its hills - circular&lt;/a&gt; route offers a scenic tour of the local landscape.

Q: What kind of historical sites can I see while hiking near Zalamea La Real?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore ancient megalithic structures on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144829416/dolmens-of-el-pozuelo-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens of El Pozuelo Route&lt;/a&gt;, which leads to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens of El Pozuelo&lt;/a&gt;. Other trails follow the historic Río Tinto river valley, often along old railway lines, like the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144803253/on-the-banks-of-the-riotinto-along-the-old-railway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Riotinto along the old railway&lt;/a&gt;, offering glimpses into the area's mining past.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Zalamea La Real?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144829416/dolmens-of-el-pozuelo-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens of El Pozuelo Route&lt;/a&gt;, at 6.9 km, is an easy path that explores ancient megalithic structures and is generally well-suited for a family outing. Many of the 14 easy trails provide gentle terrain and shorter distances.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Zalamea La Real?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Zalamea La Real, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied landscapes, the historical points of interest, and the well-maintained paths along the Río Tinto.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking trails around Zalamea La Real?
A: For those seeking a longer adventure, the region offers more extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984221964/berrocal-el-madrono-circular-by-the-rio-tinto-and-arroyo-del-gallego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berrocal - El Madroño – circular by the Río Tinto and Arroyo del Gallego&lt;/a&gt; trail is a challenging 23.4 km route that takes over 6 hours to complete, providing a significant long-distance hiking experience.

Q: What natural attractions can I discover near Zalamea La Real?
A: Beyond the trails, the area boasts several natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4402997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Río Tinto&lt;/a&gt;, explore the unique landscape around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300427" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Hierro&lt;/a&gt;, or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calabazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. These highlights offer additional points of interest to complement your hike.

Q: Is it possible to hike along the Río Tinto river?
A: Yes, hiking along the Río Tinto river is a defining feature of the area. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144750763/rio-tinto-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Tinto Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144803253/on-the-banks-of-the-riotinto-along-the-old-railway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;On the banks of the Riotinto along the old railway&lt;/a&gt; follow its unique, reddish waters, offering scenic views and insights into the region's mining heritage.

Q: Are there any specific highlights or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Absolutely. Keep an eye out for the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765536" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens of El Pozuelo&lt;/a&gt;, which are a significant archaeological site. The trails also pass by the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corta Atalaya Mine&lt;/a&gt;, an impressive open-pit mine, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301118" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge over the Odiel River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Zalamea La Real?
A: The best time to hike in Zalamea La Real is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter, though hiking is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: Are there trails suitable for beginner hikers in Zalamea La Real?
A: Beginner hikers will find plenty of suitable options. With 14 easy routes available, many trails offer gentle gradients and shorter distances, making them ideal for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed walk. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144829416/dolmens-of-el-pozuelo-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmens of El Pozuelo Route&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of an accessible and rewarding easy trail.
tips: One of the most populated areas of archaeological sites in Zalamea La Real. This site was excavated in the middle of the 20th century and was dated between 3000 and 2500 BC. c.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Dolmens of El Pozuelo circular trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike typically takes around 1 hour and 53 minutes to complete. However, allow extra time to explore the fascinating megalithic sites along the way.

faqs.question: Is the Dolmens of El Pozuelo circular trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as easy, but it does involve varied terrain with some ascents and descents, including narrow paths through bushes. While generally accessible, a moderate level of fitness will make the experience more enjoyable.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular trail?
faqs.answer: You'll hike through a wooded area with varied terrain, including steep slopes, ravines, and streams. Expect both descending and ascending sections, with some paths being narrow and running through bushes. The ground can include slate, porphyry slabs, and flagstones.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The main attraction is the concentration of megalithic structures, especially the ancient dolmens. You'll pass by the Dolmens of El Pozuelo, including notable ones like Dolmen No. 7 with its clover-shaped layout, and Dolmen No. 3 and 4. The trail also features a prehistoric copper mine and remnants of a fortified settlement, all set within a natural environment of cistus, pine trees, cork oaks, and holm oaks.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Dolmens of El Pozuelo circular trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Andalusia, with milder temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cooler, potentially wetter weather.

faqs.question: Is the Dolmens of El Pozuelo circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially around archaeological sites and to protect local wildlife and vegetation.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there is no mention of permits or entrance fees required to access the Dolmens of El Pozuelo circular trail or the archaeological site itself. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Dolmens of El Pozuelo circular trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available near the trailhead. Be aware that some access roads to the area can be challenging for vehicles with low suspension, suggesting potentially rugged conditions. Specific parking areas are usually signposted locally.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Information regarding direct public transport to the trailhead is limited. The trail is located near Zalamea la Real, Huelva. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Zalamea la Real and then plan for a taxi or private transport to the specific trailhead if you don't have a car.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is in a natural and historical area. For amenities like cafes, restaurants, or accommodation, you would need to visit the nearby town of Zalamea la Real. It's best to bring your own water and snacks for the hike.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 26% of this route also passes through sections of the Bajada a Pantano "El Vela" trail.
